jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase itself
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, became a cultural touchstone

jokes.text: The shampoo specifically said, 'No more tears.' A lovely promise...but one beyond the powers of a mere shampoo.
jokes.setup: Burns references Johnson's Baby Shampoo slogan about tears (meaning eye irritation)
jokes.punchline: Burns reveals he means tears of sadness, not eye tears - the double meaning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay on the double meaning of 'tears' - perfectly fits Burns' melancholy and wit

jokes.text: I dreamed of grand slam home runs...and wiping out nations with the stroke of a pen.
jokes.setup: Burns nostalgically recalls childhood dreams
jokes.punchline: The casual juxtaposition of baseball and genocide
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - the matter-of-fact delivery of horrific ambition alongside innocent childhood dreams

jokes.text: Controlled nuclear fission is a demanding mistress.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining his relationship with the power plant
jokes.punchline: The personification of nuclear power as a romantic relationship
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Burns' relationship with power and control

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's frustration at something
jokes.punchline: The catchphrase again
jokes.explanation: Second use of the signature catchphrase

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer reacting to something
jokes.punchline: Third D'oh
jokes.explanation: Third iteration - still effective but diminishing returns

jokes.text: All employees got some...in exchange for waiving certain constitutional rights.
jokes.setup: Explaining how Homer got stock in the plant
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation that constitutional rights were traded for stock
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about corporate overreach - perfectly casual delivery makes it funnier

jokes.text: The book says to make small talk...before getting down to business. - Everybody alive? - Yeah. - Like any sports? - Sure. - Ever go dancing? - Not anymore. - We should get together sometime. - That'd be great. There. Now we trust each other.
jokes.setup: Stockbroker mechanically following a customer service script
jokes.punchline: The robotic delivery and Homer's declaration that they now trust each other
jokes.explanation: Good satire of forced customer service interactions

jokes.text: Sell! Sell! Sell! Whoo-hoo! Twenty-five dollars!
jokes.setup: Homer learning his stock is worth only $25
jokes.punchline: His disproportionate excitement over the tiny amount
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - his childlike joy at small wins

jokes.text: Your father's stock is worth $5,200!
jokes.setup: Family learning about the stock value
jokes.punchline: The irony that Homer sold for $25 when it's now worth thousands
jokes.explanation: Sets up the dramatic irony of Homer's premature sale

jokes.text: Wow! 5,200 smackers!
jokes.setup: Bart reacting to the stock value
jokes.punchline: His use of dated slang 'smackers'
jokes.explanation: Bart's anachronistic slang is mildly amusing

jokes.text: Buy low, sell high-- that's my motto. I may quit my job at the power plant...and become a full-time...stock market guy.
jokes.setup: Homer bragging about his stock success
jokes.punchline: His delusion about becoming a stock trader based on one $25 sale
jokes.explanation: Homer's overconfidence based on minimal success is very in-character

jokes.text: My English is not perfect, but I have to tell you...your beer is like swill to us. Only a swine would drink this beer.
jokes.setup: German politely apologizing for imperfect English
jokes.punchline: Then delivering a brutal insult in perfect English
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - the polite setup makes the harsh insult funnier

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry. We'll have enough left to buy the Cleveland Browns.
jokes.setup: German confirming they have enough money
jokes.punchline: The casual reference to buying an NFL team as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the Germans' wealth scale

jokes.text: Homer's probably buying some magic beans with it.
jokes.setup: Family discussing what to spend money on
jokes.punchline: Marge's resigned expectation of Homer's poor judgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge line - loving but realistic about Homer's judgment

jokes.text: I sold it all for 25 bucks!
jokes.setup: Family thinking Homer spent $5,200 on beer
jokes.punchline: The revelation he only had $25 to spend
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - makes the family's anger even more justified

jokes.text: - Oww! - Come on, everybody. It makes you feel better.
jokes.setup: Homer getting hit
jokes.punchline: Bart encouraging everyone to hit Homer because it feels good
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons family violence played for laughs

jokes.text: It started as an eye tuck, but the stock kept climbing.
jokes.setup: Lenny got extensive plastic surgery
jokes.punchline: He kept adding procedures as the stock price rose
jokes.explanation: Clever connection between stock performance and cosmetic surgery addiction

jokes.text: You'll see the Statue of Liberty wearing lederhosen...before you'll see Germans running my plant!
jokes.setup: Burns declaring he won't sell to Germans
jokes.punchline: The specific absurd image of the Statue of Liberty in German shorts
jokes.explanation: Vivid, absurd imagery typical of Burns' dramatic flair

jokes.text: So I can look Uncle Fritz square in the monocle...and say, 'Nein.!'
jokes.setup: Burns explaining why he's meeting with Germans
jokes.punchline: The German stereotype references and dramatic delivery
jokes.explanation: Burns' theatrical nature and cultural stereotyping

jokes.text: I grudgingly accept.
jokes.setup: Burns previously declaring he'd never sell
jokes.punchline: His immediate capitulation when offered money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns hypocrisy - principles versus money

jokes.text: Hans and Fritz-- Why, that's just John and Frank.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to make German names sound American
jokes.punchline: The obviously false equivalence
jokes.explanation: Burns trying to spin the obvious German takeover

jokes.text: Look at all those worried faces...except for Lenny. He looks great.
jokes.setup: Workers worried about losing jobs
jokes.punchline: Visual contrast with Lenny's plastic surgery making him look happy
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Lenny's plastic surgery subplot

jokes.text: Perhaps I remind you of lovable Sergeant Schultz...on Hogan's Heroes.
jokes.setup: German trying to seem nonthreatening
jokes.punchline: Reference to the bumbling Nazi character from TV
jokes.explanation: Self-aware cultural reference about German stereotypes

jokes.text: Uh... me. Right here. I'm drunk right now.
jokes.setup: Horst asking if there are any alcoholics
jokes.punchline: Barney's enthusiastic, honest admission of current intoxication
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment - his honesty about alcoholism is both funny and sad

jokes.text: Hey, maybe I'll marry Elizabeth Taylor.
jokes.setup: Homer jealous of Barney's treatment
jokes.punchline: His non-sequitur about marrying Elizabeth Taylor
jokes.explanation: Random Homer non-sequitur showing his jealousy

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he's the safety inspector they want to talk to
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation of realization
jokes.explanation: Fourth D'oh - still effective for the realization moment

jokes.text: You looken sharpen todayen, mein herr.
jokes.setup: Smithers trying to speak German to impress new owners
jokes.punchline: His mangled attempt at German grammar and pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Smithers' desperate attempt to curry favor with new management

jokes.text: He was good to his mother. Yes, but I couldn't understand a word that man said. 'Mr. Burns...hound dog.' Stop it. You're killing me, sir.
jokes.setup: Burns giving away Elvis photo, praising Elvis
jokes.punchline: Burns' confusion about Elvis lyrics mixed with Smithers' forced laughter
jokes.explanation: Burns' age showing through cultural disconnect with Elvis

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer learning about German work ethic
jokes.punchline: Fifth D'oh of realization/frustration
jokes.explanation: Fifth D'oh - diminishing returns but still characteristically Homer

jokes.text: If I'm not a safety 'whatchamajigger,' I'm nothing.
jokes.setup: Homer worrying about his identity
jokes.punchline: His inability to remember his own job title while claiming it's his identity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer paradox - can't remember job title but says it defines him

jokes.text: Fit, damn you! Fit!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to assemble something
jokes.punchline: His frustrated outburst at inanimate objects
jokes.explanation: Homer's relationship with mechanical objects

jokes.text: Hey, you! Stop being... so unsafe! Smitty... safen up.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to do his safety inspector job
jokes.punchline: His awkward, made-up safety terminology
jokes.explanation: Homer's incompetence showing through invented safety jargon

jokes.text: No. I must have phrased that badly. My English is inelegant. I meant to say...may we have a brief, friendly chat? No. Failed again. 'We request the pleasure of your company...for a free exchange of ideas.' Nooo!
jokes.setup: German trying increasingly formal language to talk to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's consistent 'No' despite increasingly polite requests
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of politeness meeting Homer's stubborn refusal

jokes.text: Well, uh... I wish the candy machine...wasn't so picky about taking beat-up dollar bills...because...a lot of workers really like candy.
jokes.setup: Homer asked about safety initiatives
jokes.punchline: His completely irrelevant candy machine complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - completely missing the point but focusing on candy

jokes.text: We understand, Homer. After all, we are from the land of chocolate.
jokes.setup: Homer's candy machine complaint
jokes.punchline: German's diplomatic response referencing Germany as 'land of chocolate'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the famous chocolate land fantasy sequence

jokes.text: Wow... chocolate, half-price!
jokes.setup: Homer fantasizing about land of chocolate
jokes.punchline: His focus on the discount rather than free chocolate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - even in fantasy land, he's bargain hunting

jokes.text: That was ten minutes ago.
jokes.setup: Homer lost in chocolate daydream during meeting
jokes.punchline: Reveals how long Homer was mentally absent
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery revealing Homer's complete mental absence

jokes.text: Sure, they've made mistakes in the past, but that's why pencils have erasers.
jokes.setup: Homer saying Germans aren't so bad
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of German historical atrocities with pencil eraser metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Homer's naive optimism applied to genocide

jokes.text: Simpson, Homer. That is all.
jokes.setup: Long list of layoffs expected
jokes.punchline: Only Homer gets fired from entire workforce
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - expected mass layoffs becomes just Homer

jokes.text: Instead of buying comic books, I read them in the store.
jokes.setup: Family discussing money-saving measures
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual admission of essentially stealing entertainment
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart moral flexibility presented innocently

jokes.text: When I get you, I'm gonna use your head for a bucket...and paint my house with your brains.
jokes.setup: Moe realizing he's been prank called
jokes.punchline: His grotesquely specific threat of violence
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe - creatively violent threat with domestic imagery

jokes.text: Ah, the mirthless laugh of the damned.
jokes.setup: Hearing laughter in Moe's Tavern
jokes.punchline: Burns' pretentious description of bar patrons' laughter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line - pretentious, dramatic, and darkly poetic description of working-class joy

jokes.text: Watch me blend in. Barkeep, some cheap domestic beer...for me and my buddy here.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to act like a common person
jokes.punchline: His obviously upper-class attempt at working-class slang
jokes.explanation: Burns' complete inability to relate to common people

jokes.text: Does your money ever hug you...when you come home at night? Why, no. And does it ever say 'I love you'? No, it doesn't.
jokes.setup: Homer challenging Burns about money vs. love
jokes.punchline: Burns' honest admission that money doesn't provide emotional connection
jokes.explanation: Homer's rare moment of philosophical clarity vs. Burns

jokes.text: That's because I forgot to stamp it!
jokes.setup: Burns not remembering Bart's letter
jokes.punchline: Bart's admission he never actually sent it
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart - plans revenge but forgets the basics

jokes.text: Who'd have thought a nuclear plant could be such a death trap?
jokes.setup: Germans discovering plant's safety issues
jokes.punchline: The ironic surprise that a nuclear plant might be dangerous
jokes.explanation: Dark irony about nuclear safety from efficiency-minded Germans

jokes.text: Desperate, eh? Advantage: Burns.
jokes.setup: Germans saying they're desperate to sell
jokes.punchline: Burns' internal monologue spoken aloud like a chess game
jokes.explanation: Burns treating business negotiation like a game with internal commentary

jokes.text: Ooh, the Germans are mad at me. I'm so scared. Ooh, the Germans! Stop it. Stop that. The Germans are coming after me. Stop the pretending you are scared game. They're so big. Stop it, Mr. Burns. Protect me from the Germans! Stop it! Get out! This is my office now!
jokes.setup: Germans warning Burns they're not all sunshine
jokes.punchline: Burns' childish mock-fear escalating until Smithers snaps
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Burns pushing Smithers to his breaking point

jokes.text: Smithers, I keep my friends close...and my enemies even closer.
jokes.setup: Smithers asking why rehire Homer
jokes.punchline: Burns quoting The Godfather about strategy
jokes.explanation: Classic movie quote applied to Burns' petty revenge

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! I got my job back!
jokes.setup: Homer being rehired as part of Burns' revenge scheme
jokes.punchline: His oblivious joy while being manipulated
jokes.explanation: Homer's innocent happiness contrasted with Burns' sinister plotting
